description: The procedure code is inconsistent with the modifier used or a required modifier is missing.
resolution: Review the procedure code and modifier combination. Ensure the correct modifier is appended. Resubmit with the appropriate modifier or corrected procedure code.

description: The diagnosis is inconsistent with the procedure.
resolution: Verify the diagnosis code supports the medical necessity of the procedure. Correct the diagnosis code if incorrect or obtain additional documentation supporting the medical necessity.

description: Claim/service lacks information or has submission/billing error(s).
resolution: Review the claim for missing or incorrect information. Common issues include missing NDC codes, incorrect place of service, or missing referring provider. Correct and resubmit.

description: Exact duplicate claim/service.
resolution: Verify that this claim was not previously submitted and paid. If it is a legitimate duplicate, no action is needed. If it should have been paid, resubmit with corrected information to differentiate it.

description: This care may be covered by another payer per coordination of benefits.
resolution: Verify the patient's other insurance coverage. Submit the claim to the primary payer first and include the EOB when billing the secondary payer.

description: Charges are covered under a capitation agreement/managed care plan.
resolution: Verify the patient's managed care enrollment. If the service is not capitated, submit documentation showing the service is carved out of the capitation arrangement.

description: Expenses incurred after coverage terminated.
resolution: Verify the patient's coverage dates. If coverage was active on the date of service, submit proof of coverage. If not, bill the patient or secondary insurance.

description: The time limit for filing has expired.
resolution: Check the payer's timely filing limit. If the claim was filed timely, submit proof of timely filing (e.g., original submission confirmation). If not, write off the balance.

description: Charge exceeds fee schedule/maximum allowable or contracted/legislated fee arrangement.
resolution: Review the contracted or fee schedule rate. The allowed amount has been applied. The remaining balance may not be billed to the patient if participating.

description: These are non-covered services because this is not deemed a medical necessity by the payer.
resolution: Review the medical necessity criteria. If documentation supports necessity, submit an appeal with clinical records, peer-reviewed literature, or LCD/NCD references.

description: Non-covered charge(s). At least one Remark Code must be provided.
resolution: Review the accompanying RARC code for specific denial reason. Address the specific issue identified in the remark code.

description: The benefit for this service is included in the payment/allowance for another service/procedure that has already been adjudicated.
resolution: Review the CCI edits and bundling rules. If the services are truly distinct, resubmit with appropriate modifier (59, XE, XP, XS, or XU) with supporting documentation.

description: Claim/service not covered by this payer/contractor. You must send the claim/service to the correct payer/contractor.
resolution: Verify the patient's insurance information and submit to the correct payer. Check for Medicare Advantage enrollment or Medicaid managed care assignment.

description: Benefit maximum for this time period or occurrence has been reached.
resolution: Verify the benefit limits with the payer. If the limit has been met, inform the patient. If the limit is incorrect, submit an appeal with documentation of the benefit structure.

description: This (these) diagnosis(es) is (are) not covered. Note: Refer to the 835 Healthcare Policy Identification Segment (loop 2110 Service Payment Information REF), if present.
resolution: Verify the diagnosis code is correct. If the diagnosis is not covered under the patient's plan, inform the patient. Consider whether an alternative covered diagnosis is applicable.

description: Precertification/authorization/notification absent.
resolution: Obtain prior authorization if still possible. If authorization was obtained, submit proof of authorization. If retrospective authorization is available, obtain it and resubmit.

description: Precertification/authorization/notification exceeded.
resolution: Verify the authorized units or dates. If additional services were medically necessary, request an authorization extension or submit an appeal.

description: This service/equipment/drug is not covered under the patient's current benefit plan.
resolution: Verify the patient's benefit plan details. If the service should be covered, contact the payer for clarification. If not covered, advise the patient of financial responsibility.

description: This procedure or procedure/modifier combination is not compatible with another procedure or procedure/modifier combination provided on the same day according to the National Correct Coding Initiative.
resolution: Review CCI edits for the code combination. If services are distinct, add the appropriate modifier (59/X modifiers). Ensure documentation supports the separate services.

description: An attachment/other documentation is required to adjudicate this claim/service.
resolution: Submit the required documentation such as operative notes, medical records, or prior authorization documentation. Check the RARC code for specifics on what documentation is needed.

description: Deductible Amount
resolution: The patient's deductible has not been met. Bill the patient for the deductible amount. Verify the deductible amount and status with the payer if there is a discrepancy.

description: Coinsurance Amount
resolution: The patient is responsible for the coinsurance portion. Bill the patient for the coinsurance amount per their benefit plan.

description: Co-payment Amount
resolution: The patient is responsible for the copay. Ensure the copay amount was collected at the time of service. If not, bill the patient.

description: This is a non-covered service because it is a routine/preventive exam or a diagnostic/screening procedure done in conjunction with a routine/preventive exam.
resolution: Verify the diagnosis code. If the service was performed for a diagnostic reason, resubmit with the appropriate diagnosis code. If preventive, check the patient's preventive benefits.

description: Non-covered charge(s). Patient is responsible.
resolution: Verify the service is truly non-covered. If covered, appeal. If non-covered, bill the patient ensuring proper ABN was obtained for Medicare patients.

description: Missing/incomplete/invalid prior authorization number.
resolution: Obtain the correct prior authorization number from the payer and resubmit the claim with the authorization number in the appropriate field.

description: Patient ineligible for this service on the date of service.
resolution: Verify the patient's eligibility for the date of service. Contact the payer to confirm enrollment and covered benefits.

description: The number of days or units of service exceeds our acceptable maximum.
resolution: Review the units billed against the payer's policy. If units are correct, submit an appeal with documentation supporting medical necessity for the additional units.

description: This decision was based on a National Coverage Determination (NCD). An NCD provides a coverage determination as to whether a particular item or service is covered.
resolution: Review the referenced NCD for coverage criteria. Ensure documentation meets NCD requirements. Appeal if criteria are met but the claim was denied.

description: This service is only covered when performed as part of a specific program or treatment.
resolution: Verify the service was performed within the required program parameters. Submit documentation of program enrollment or treatment plan.

description: Missing NDC or invalid NDC code for the drug.
resolution: Verify the correct 11-digit NDC code and resubmit. Ensure the NDC matches the drug administered.

description: Invalid or missing place of service code.
resolution: Verify the place of service code matches where the service was rendered. Correct the POS code and resubmit.

description: The provider is not eligible to bill for this service on the date of service.
resolution: Verify the provider's enrollment and credentialing with the payer. If recently credentialed, submit proof of enrollment.

description: This service was processed based on a previously adjudicated claim.
resolution: Review the original claim adjudication. If the original was processed incorrectly, request a reopening or submit a corrected claim.

description: If you do not agree with what we approved for these services, you may appeal our decision.
resolution: If you disagree with the payment determination, file a formal appeal within the required timeframe with supporting documentation.

description: Secondary payment cannot be considered without the identity of or payment information from the primary payer.
resolution: Submit the primary payer's EOB/ERA with the secondary claim. Include the primary payer's payment and adjustment information.

description: The claim information has also been forwarded to Medicaid for review.
resolution: No immediate action required. The claim has been crossover to Medicaid for potential additional payment. Monitor for Medicaid remittance.

description: Your claim contains incomplete and/or invalid information, and no appeal rights are afforded because the claim is unprocessable.
resolution: Review the claim for errors. Correct all missing or invalid information and resubmit as a new claim (not an appeal). Common issues: missing NPI, invalid TIN, missing demographics.

description: This decision was based on a Local Coverage Determination (LCD).
resolution: Review the referenced LCD for specific coverage criteria. Ensure documentation meets all LCD requirements before resubmitting or appealing.

description: Missing or incomplete service facility address.
resolution: Verify and include the complete service facility address including name, street, city, state, and ZIP on the claim form.

description: Procedure code billed is not correct/valid for the services billed or the date of service billed.
resolution: Verify the procedure code is valid for the date of service. Check for code deletions or replacements. Resubmit with the correct procedure code.

description: This provider type/specialty may not bill this service.
resolution: Verify that the provider's specialty/taxonomy code is eligible to bill this service. If incorrect, update the provider's enrollment information with the payer.

description: Consult the provider/supplier for the explanation of this adjustment.
resolution: Review internal records for the reason behind the adjustment. Communicate with the payer if the reason is unclear.

description: Alert: You may not appeal this decision.
resolution: Review the denial carefully. If a formal appeal is not available, consider requesting a reopening or submitting a new claim if there were correctable errors.

description: The impact of prior payer(s) adjudication including payments and/or adjustments.
resolution: This is informational for coordination of benefits. Review the primary payer's adjudication and ensure the secondary claim reflects the primary payment correctly.

description: This provider was not certified/eligible to be paid for this procedure/service on this date of service.
resolution: Verify provider enrollment and credentialing status with the payer for the date of service. If recently enrolled, provide effective date documentation.

description: Patient is enrolled in a Hospice.
resolution: Verify the patient's hospice enrollment status. If the service is unrelated to the terminal diagnosis, submit with the GW modifier and appropriate documentation.

description: This procedure code was added/changed because it more accurately describes the services rendered.
resolution: Review the code substitution. If you disagree with the payer's code change, submit documentation supporting your original code selection.

description: This service was denied/reduced based on the results of a pre-payment or post-payment review.
resolution: Request the specific findings from the review. Submit additional documentation to support the medical necessity and appropriateness of the service.
